sections.paragraph:
ἀναζάω 1 aor. ἀνέζησα (Nicander [II b.c.] in epic form-ζώω Fgm. 70, 8 [Athen. 4, 11 p. 133d]; Chariton 3, 8, 9; Artem. 4, 82; Paradox. Flor. 6; CIG 2566, 2; JosAs 19:3 cod. A for ἀνεζωοπύρησαν; Dssm., LO 75f [LAE 94ff ]; Nägeli 47; DELG s.v. ζώω) ‘come (back) to life’.

① to come to life out of a condition of death

ⓐ lit. be resurrected (so in the places cited above) of the dead Rv 20:5 t.r., an Erasmian rdg. without known ms. evidence; s. RBorger, TRu 52, ’87, 57 (ParJer 7:18); of Christ Ro 14:9 v.l.

ⓑ fig. be alive again, of one morally and spiritually dead ὁ υἱός μου νεκρὸς ἦν καὶ ἀνέζησεν Lk 15:24 (v.l. ἔζησεν); 32 v.l. (ἔζησεν in the text).

② to function after being dormant, spring into life (with loss of the force of ἀνά; s. ἀναβλέπω 2aβ) ἡ ἁμαρτία ἀνέζησεν sin became alive Ro 7:9.—M-M. TW.

aor. aor. = aorist

Nicander Nicander , III/II b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

Chariton Chariton , scriptor eroticus (writer of romances) I–II a.d.—List 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

CIG CIG = Corpus Inscriptionum Graecarum—List 3

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Dssm. Dssm. = ADeissmann, various works—List 6

LO LO = Licht vom Osten—s. Dssm., LO in List 6

LAE LAE , s. Dssm. LO—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

t.r. t.r. = textus receptus

rdg. rdg. = reading(s)

  1. ms. = manuscript(s)

TRu TRu = Theologische Rundschau—List 6

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 62
